next_steps: Immediate CT angiography of the neck to evaluate for vascular anomalies, pseudoaneurysm, or high-riding carotid artery before any further intervention.
Multidisciplinary team planning (Otolaryngology, Vascular Surgery, and Interventional Radiology) to manage potential massive hemorrhage during biopsy.
Excisional biopsy in a controlled operating room environment with airway protection and vascular surgical backup.
Comprehensive histopathological assessment including H&E, myogenic markers (MyoD1, Myogenin, Desmin), and FISH for FOXO1.
Systemic staging with PET/CT and cross-sectional imaging of the chest and abdomen to evaluate for occult malignancy or distant spread.
Consultation with Oncology/Pathology for rapid review and molecular analysis (NGS) of the biopsy specimen.
